title: Subject–Verb Agreement
subtitle: Finding the real subject when the sentence tries to hide it
intro: In a short sentence, agreement is obvious. The moment you add a prepositional phrase or a relative clause between the subject and its verb, agreement errors multiply. Examiners count every 'the list of candidates are...' against Grammatical Accuracy. The fix is a single habit: before writing the verb, strip away the modifiers and locate the head noun.

rules.heading: Interrupters do not change the subject
rules.body: 'The list of new candidates IS long' — the head is 'list', not 'candidates'.

rules.heading: 'There' takes the number of what follows
rules.body: 'There IS a problem' → 'There ARE several problems'.

rules.heading: Collective nouns: one unit or many members?
rules.body: British English accepts both: 'The team IS' (unit) / 'The team ARE' (individuals). Writing Task 2 safer with singular unless you mean the members.

rules.heading: Either/neither … or / each / every / none
rules.body: 'Each student HAS a desk'; 'Either option IS viable'; 'Neither of the proposals WAS accepted'; 'None of the data IS conclusive' (modern usage).

examples.sentence: The number of international students has risen steadily.
examples.note: 'The number of X' takes singular verb.

examples.sentence: A number of policies have failed to reduce inequality.
examples.note: 'A number of' takes plural. Do not confuse with 'the number of'.

examples.sentence: Neither the government nor the opposition appears willing to compromise.
examples.note: Singular subjects joined by 'neither…nor' → singular verb.

examples.sentence: Every one of my classmates has a different study strategy.
examples.note: 'Every one' is singular despite 'of my classmates'.

examples.sentence: Twenty percent of the population lives below the poverty line.
examples.note: Fraction/percentage takes the number of the noun it refers to: 'population' → singular.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers handle the tricky plural-looking singulars and singular-looking plurals with ease: 'The data show' (formal plural of datum) or 'The data shows' (modern acceptable singular), 'politics is' (the field) vs. 'her politics are' (her views), 'statistics is' (the subject) vs. 'the statistics are' (the numbers). Control of these micro-choices signals a reader who respects conventions.

common_errors.fix: 'Everyone' singular → 'Everyone HAS their opinion' (singular they allowed).

practice.title: Find the disagreement
practice.instruction: Click the verb that does not agree with its subject.

items.prompt: The impact of digital platforms on young minds [A:are] becoming [B:impossible] to ignore.
There [A:is] several reasons [B:why] the proposal failed.
Each of the applicants [A:were] required to submit [B:a portfolio].
Neither the committee nor the director [A:are] willing to comment [B:on the matter].
The number of remote workers [A:have] doubled [B:since 2020].
A wide range of proposals [A:have] been submitted [B:for review].
items.explanation: 'A wide range of' can take plural when focus is on items.

items.prompt: Everyone in the workshop [A:have] their own perspective [B:on the issue].
Ten years [A:is] a long time [B:in politics].
items.explanation: Period of time treated as a single unit → singular.

practice.title: Agree with the subject
practice.instruction: Type 'is / are / was / were / has / have / does / do' to match.

items.prompt: The media often ___ guilty of sensationalism.
items.explanation: 'Media' accepts both; singular is now more common.

items.prompt: Statistics ___ a subject I always struggled with.
The statistics on this chart ___ self-explanatory.
One of the biggest challenges facing educators today ___ funding.
Neither of those options ___ acceptable to the board.
A majority of voters ___ in favour of the amendment.
items.explanation: 'A majority of + plural noun' → plural verb.

items.prompt: News about the merger ___ spreading quickly.
Fifty per cent of the budget ___ allocated to healthcare.
A survey of 2,000 residents ___ that housing costs are the top concern.
The number of applications ___ every year.
A number of applications ___ been rejected this month.
Either the teacher or her students ___ wrong.
Every one of those policies ___ its own drawbacks.
My family ___ divided on the question of relocation.
items.explanation: Collective noun referring to individual members → plural acceptable.

items.prompt: There ___ no reason to postpone the decision.
The committee ___ announced its decision this morning.
